date_notes: Date of Premier's social media video address. Global News coverage dated approximately February 1 2024. Bills tabled October 31 2024; passed December 4 2024 (EVT-033).
title_en: Premier announces restrictions on gender-affirming care and school policies for transgender youth
title_fr: La première ministre annonce des restrictions sur les soins d'affirmation de genre pour les jeunes transgenres
description_en: The Premier of Alberta announces via social media video address a suite of policies affecting transgender youth: restrictions on puberty blockers and hormone therapy for those 15 and under (unless already in treatment); parental notification and consent requirements for name and pronoun changes at school; opt-in parental consent for sex education and gender identity instruction; and limitations on transgender athletes in female competitive sports. The Premier states the policies 'struck the right balance,' are 'supportive,' and that the government 'consulted very broadly' without naming consultees. Medical researchers from Simon Fraser University and the University of Toronto respond that restricting puberty blockers increases suicide risk in trans youth. The prohibition on bottom surgery for under-18s restated an existing Canada-wide restriction.
opposition_response: Alberta NDP characterized the pronoun policy as 'mean-spirited' and a distraction from provincial issues.

relations.framing_en: The premier described the gender-affirming-care restrictions as 'supportive' and the product of broad consultation. Named medical researchers (Travers, Simon Fraser University; Brennan, University of Toronto) state that restricting puberty blockers for trans youth increases suicide risk.

verification_notes: Confirm exact date of social media video. R-044 URL needs HTTP check. Bottom surgery 18+ restriction: confirm Canada-wide standard pre-dated announcement per WPATH standards.

update_log.change: Added R-099 (Tyee/Conversation Nov 2024 — researcher documentation of harm to LGBTQ2S+ families from upcoming legislation).
